name: Rote / Nemberala (T-Land) / 羅地島 / Pulau Rote - Nemberala (Besialu)
gps_location: Nemberala village, southwest Rote Island, approx. -10.93, 122.83; T-Land reef approx. 500m offshore from Nemberala beach [uncertain exact coords]
wave_type: Long left-hand reef point break over flat coral reef; one of the longest left-handers in Indonesia. Three main named sections - The Peak (or The Point), The Pyramid (Steeple), and The Mountain (Magic Mountain) - plus Inner Tubes section.
skill_level: Intermediate to advanced (accommodates all levels on smaller days; expert on solid swells). One of the most user-friendly world-class waves in Indo.
wave_height_range: 2-15 ft (works from 1ft swell up to triple overhead 15ft). Sweet spot 3-8 ft.
bottom_type: reef (flat coral reef, relatively forgiving compared to other Indo reef breaks but shallow at low tide)
best_season: Dry season May-October; peak May-August with consistent SSW groundswells from the Roaring 40s and offshore ESE trades dawn to dusk.
best_tide: All tides (T-Land's hallmark - works at all tides); caution at very low tide due to exposed coral. Mid to high tide most forgiving for beginners.
best_wind: Offshore E to SE trade winds (blows offshore dawn to dusk in dry season)
swell_consistency_rating: 5 (extremely consistent - surfable waves nearly every day in dry season)
wave_length_meters: Up to 400+ meters on right tide and swell (sources cite 600-1200 ft / ~180-360m on bigger days)
wave_speed_rating: medium (rippable carving walls; not freight-train fast - more playful and open-faced with occasional barrel sections)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Medium - multiple takeoff zones spread crowd; outer Peak has steeper drops on larger swells, inside sections more mellow.
secondary_peaks: Three distinct sections (The Peak / Pyramid / Mountain) plus Inner Tubes section. Multiple peaks help spread crowds; The Bommie just north of T-Land is a short fast right on low tide / big swell.
peak_crowd_level: 2 (busiest wave on Rote but multiple peaks and 400m+ length spread crowds; very manageable compared to Bali)
access: Bali (DPS) -> Kupang (KOE) ~1.5hr flight on Wings/Citilink/Batik Air, then either: (a) 30-min Wings Air flight KOE->RTI same-day, or (b) Express Bahari fast ferry 2 hrs (daily 9am), or (c) slow ferry to Ba'a (4hr, arriving evening). Then 45-60 min road transfer to Nemberala. Total: 1-2 days door-to-door. Boat charters also reach Rote on extended Indo charter routes.
paddle_out_method: T-Land reef sits ~500m offshore. Standard method is water taxi / outrigger from Nemberala harbour or beach (most surfers choose boat). Self-paddle possible but long, involving wading across shallow inner reef at low tide and paddling deeper sections - allow 20-30 min.
recommended_session_time: All-day surfable in dry season due to consistent offshore winds; dawn patrol best for glass and fewer surfers; late afternoon also classic.
boat_charter_required: Not required for accessing T-Land from Nemberala (water taxi standard, self-paddle possible). Boat charters via Bali->Rote are an alternative full-trip option.
combo_trip_potential: Can be combined with Sumba, Savu, West Timor and broader Nusa Tenggara island-hopping. Specialty Indo charter boats sometimes include Rote in extended routes from Bali.
hazards: Shallow flat coral reef (primary), long paddle if not taking boat, strong currents on inside sections, sun exposure during all-day sessions, remote medical facilities
marine_hazards: Sea urchins on shallow reef, occasional jellyfish, minor reef cuts common at low tide. Sharks rare. Stonefish in reef pools.
lineup_etiquette: Standard wave-priority etiquette; respect locals who get few chances to surf - let them have waves when in lineup. Multiple peaks reduce drop-in conflicts.
localism_intensity: low (Rote locals are notably welcoming and friendly; few local surfers, no aggressive crowd protection culture)
respect_protocol: Smile and greet locals, share waves with local surfers, tip your boat taxi operators, support village warungs and homestays, respect church-going Sunday culture (Rote is predominantly Christian).
medical_nearby: Limited - Nemberala has a small village clinic (Puskesmas); Ba'a (~1hr drive) has the main hospital on Rote; serious injuries require medevac back to Kupang or Bali (BIMC/Siloam)
rescue_jet_ski_available: No standing jet ski rescue service. Boat taxis on lineup provide some support; resorts may have boat backup [uncertain]
emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency 112; resort/homestay typically liaises with local clinic and Kupang air transfer; insurance medevac line essential [uncertain specific contacts]
accommodation_options: T-Land Resort (premium, in front of main peak, max 16 guests), Nemberala Beach Resort, Villa Santai, Rote Surf Resort (Lualemba Bungalows), Boa Vida Rote, Wave Haven Rote, Manduna Resort, Utopia Rote Lodge, Seed Resort, 81 Palms. Range from budget homestays to upscale resorts (~USD 50-300/night).
webcam_url: No reliable public live webcam confirmed for T-Land; Surfline/Surfnerd forecast pages available [uncertain]
annual_competition_window: No major WSL CT event; small regional/charity surf events occasionally held; Nemberala has hosted local invitationals [uncertain specifics]
community_events: No fixed weekly community surf events; resort-hosted clinics and informal gatherings during dry season [uncertain]
